name: garmin-mcp
description: Connect Claude to Garmin Connect fitness data, smart baselines, anomalies, lagged correlations, and weekly health reviews.
long_description:
This MCP server bridges Claude to your personal Garmin Connect data via the garminconnect Python library.

**Capabilities:**
- Activities: list, detail, splits, laps, heart-rate zones, weather
- Health: steps, sleep, stress, body battery, HRV, SpO2, respiration
- Smart analytics: personal baselines, anomaly detection, lagged correlations, weekly review
- Custom reports: grouped historical reports that can be saved locally and rerun
- Training: readiness, load trends (CTL/ATL/TSB), VO2 max trend, power zones
- Workouts: upload, schedule, download, bulk operations
- Body: weight, body composition, weigh-ins
- Gear, badges, challenges, nutrition logging
- Auth tools: check saved tokens, start Garmin login, request OTP only when Garmin asks, and save tokens

**First-time setup:** Choose a persistent token directory. You can leave credentials blank and ask Claude to run check_garmin_auth, then login_to_garmin. Accounts without OTP save tokens in one call. If Garmin emails you a verification code, Claude will get an mfa_required response and you can run login_to_garmin again with the OTP code.

token_path.title: Token storage directory
token_path.description: Directory where your Garmin OAuth tokens are stored. Use a persistent folder, then run the login_to_garmin tool or garmin-mcp-auth to save tokens.

garmin_email.title: Garmin email (optional)
garmin_email.description: Your Garmin Connect email address. Leave blank if you have already run garmin-mcp-auth.

garmin_password.title: Garmin password (optional)
garmin_password.description: Your Garmin Connect password. Leave blank if you have already run garmin-mcp-auth.

garmin_mfa_code.title: One-time Garmin MFA code (optional)
garmin_mfa_code.description: Paste the code Garmin emailed or sent to you when first logging in. Leave blank once tokens exist.

reports_path.title: Saved reports file
reports_path.description: Local JSON file where custom health report definitions are saved.

tools.name: check_garmin_auth
tools.description: Check whether saved Garmin tokens exist and still work

tools.name: login_to_garmin
tools.description: Log in to Garmin, request OTP only when Garmin asks for it, and save reusable tokens

tools.name: get_activity
tools.description: Get detailed information about a single activity

tools.name: get_stats
tools.description: Get daily health statistics (steps, HR, stress, body battery, sleep)

tools.name: get_sleep_data
tools.description: Get detailed sleep data including stages and score

tools.name: get_training_readiness
tools.description: Get training readiness score and contributing factors

tools.name: upload_workout
tools.description: Upload a structured workout to Garmin Connect

tools.name: get_health_baselines
tools.description: Compare recent Garmin health metrics against personal rolling baselines

tools.name: get_wellness_anomalies
tools.description: Find unusual Garmin health days compared with recent personal baselines

tools.name: get_lagged_health_correlations
tools.description: Find delayed relationships between training, sleep, stress, HRV, readiness, and recovery

tools.name: get_weekly_health_review
tools.description: Summarize the last week against the prior week with anomalies and lagged relationships

tools.name: list_health_report_metrics
tools.description: List metric keys, groupings, and aggregations available for custom health reports

tools.name: run_custom_health_report
tools.description: Run an ad hoc or saved grouped historical health report

tools.name: save_custom_health_report
tools.description: Save a reusable custom health report definition locally

tools.name: list_saved_health_reports
tools.description: List locally saved custom health reports
